title: Blotterbook — improvement backlog (paste-ready prompts)
intro: From the 2026-06-25 audit. Each item is a self-contained prompt: scope, goal, constraints, "done when".
tags: Tags: P1 do-soon · P2 durable win · P3 polish. Send them one at a time (one surface/concern per turn).
note: Committed source of truth for the engineering backlog (replaces the retired .claude/backlog.md). The admin panel shows category/effort/status only; prompt + doneNote stay here.
commitConvention: Conventional-commit titles (enables CH12 automated versioning): when committing, and ESPECIALLY when titling a PR / squash-merge, lead the title with a conventional-commit type so the version bump can be derived automatically — `feat:` (new capability → minor), `fix:` (bug fix → patch), `chore:`/`refactor:`/`docs:`/`style:`/`test:`/`perf:` (→ patch), and append `!` (e.g. `feat!:`) or a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer for a breaking change (→ major). Scope it when useful, e.g. `feat(staging): …`. Untyped/legacy titles default to a patch bump. Keep the existing Co-Authored-By and Claude-Session trailers on the commit body.
donePolicy: When an item's status is set to `done`, delete its `prompt` field and keep only the `doneNote` — the doneNote is the shipped record of what was actually done, so the original prompt is redundant and only bloats this file (the admin backlog panel never renders done-item prompts anyway). Keep `prompt` on `open` and `guardrail` items, since those are still the actionable spec. Applies retroactively: a done item should never carry a prompt.

items.title: Close the CSV→HTML injection (XSS)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `rootSym()` (`app/adapters.js:52`) now strips to `[A-Z0-9._-]` at the source, and `esc()` was applied at every sink (`app/app.js` cost table ~493, exported report ~1165, data-manager rows ~1385/1388, trade editor ~1421 — incl. `side`). Verified in-browser: `rootSym('MES')` → `MESIMGSRCXONERRORALERT1`; a stored payload renders as escaped text (no injected element, XSS never fired); legit symbols (MES/MNQ/MCL) still render; 55/55 adapter tests pass.

items.title: Add a Pages `_headers` file with CSP + security headers
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root `_headers` file applies to `/*`: CSP (`default-src 'self'`; `img-src 'self' data:` for screenshots; `connect-src 'self'`; `object-src 'none'`; `base-uri 'self'`; `form-action 'self'`; `frame-ancestors 'none'`; no `'unsafe-eval'`), plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, `Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin`, `Permissions-Policy` (camera/mic/geo/ topics off). `script-src`/`style-src` keep `'unsafe-inline'` (3 first-party inline scripts on home/ admin/changelog + inline `style=""` attrs; no build step for nonces yet — tighten via A1/A2). NOTE: `_headers` only takes effect on Cloudflare Pages, not the local static preview — confirm the response headers on the next deploy. App/demo/staging/home/admin all still render.

items.title: Replace the raw-ADMIN_KEY-to-browser flow with a short-lived signed token
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new `functions/_lib/auth.js` issues/verifies HMAC-SHA256 tokens. `/api/admin-key` now returns a short-lived signed token (TTL `ADMIN_TOKEN_TTL_SEC`, default 2h; signed with `TOKEN_SECRET`→ falls back to `ADMIN_KEY`) instead of the raw key — the raw `ADMIN_KEY` never reaches the browser. The admin page stores/sends the token (x-admin-key header for writes; `bb_staging` cookie for staging launch). `/api/status`, `/api/config`, and the `_middleware` staging gate verify it via `isAdminAuthorized()`; the raw key stays accepted server-side as a fallback (never handed to a browser). Tokens expire. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test (valid/tamper/expiry/fallback). Full E2E needs the deployed Access+KV env.

items.title: Verify the Cloudflare Access JWT signature (defense-in-depth)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `/api/admin-key` verifies the `Cf-Access-Jwt-Assertion` RS256 signature against the team JWKS (`<ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN>/cdn-cgi/access/certs`, cached 1h) plus audience (`ACCESS_AUD`), issuer, and expiry before issuing a token (`verifyAccessJwt` in `functions/_lib/auth.js`). When `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset it falls back to the prior header-presence check (route is still behind Access + the middleware gate). Verified with a Node WebCrypto test using a generated RSA keypair + mocked JWKS: a genuine token is accepted; forged-signature, absent, expired, wrong-aud, wrong-iss, and unknown-kid are all rejected. Full E2E needs the deployed Access env.

items.title: Cap per-trade screenshot size
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `dmAddShot` (`app/datamanager.js`) now auto-downscales (longest side ≤ 1600px) and re-encodes to JPEG q0.82 whenever an image exceeds a ~600 KB soft cap, flattening transparency first; images already compact are stored as-is, and if re-encoding doesn't shrink it the original is kept. Replaced the old "over 4 MB, store anyway?" confirm with a friendly post-add note. Verified in-browser: a 4.7 MB / 3000×2000 PNG → 1600px / ~73 KB JPEG, "Image optimized for local storage (4.7 MB → ~73 KB)" shown, 0 console errors.

items.title: Round out `.gitignore`
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added `.mf/` (under Cloudflare), `npm-debug.log*` + `pnpm-debug.log*` (under logs), and a new "Local-only overrides & caches" section with `*.local` + `.cache/`. No existing entries removed.

items.title: Single-source nav, footer, app-shell, and design tokens via a tiny prebuild
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `tokens.css` is the lone source of design tokens — `site.css` + `app/app.css` `@import` it, the homepage links it (3 `:root` blocks → 1; verified `--accent`/bg resolve on info, home, and app pages). `partials/nav.html` + `partials/footer.html` are injected by `scripts/build-includes.mjs` (idempotent; `active=KEY` highlights the current link) into the four info pages via `<!-- include:nav/footer -->` markers. Footers were unified (each had drifted — self-link omission + a howto-only line, which was relocated into howto's body). Homepage + admin keep bespoke nav/footer by design (different links/CTA/scroll-spy), sharing only tokens. Verified in-browser: nav active states, unified footer, correct theme, 0 console errors; build re-run reports 0 changes. **Not done (deferred):** the app *shell* (`app/index.html`↔`staging.html` near-duplicate markup) is still hand-kept — fold into a shared shell partial in a follow-up (pairs naturally with A3).

items.title: Split `app/app.js` into concern-scoped script files
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `app.js` → 7 ordered classic scripts: `core.js` (globals/helpers/metrics/format/cost/ refdata), `render.js` (cards/curve/calendar/advanced/break-even + scope/filter driver), `data.js` (import/demo/filters/journal/session/setup), `ui.js` (panels/staging flair/file-dl), `export.js` (report), `datamanager.js` (manage-data modal), `main.js` (event wiring + boot, loaded LAST). Cut at section boundaries in original order, so concatenation == original (no reordering). Verified: all 7 pass `node --check`; main app (initial state, 9 brokers), demo (loaded, curve), and staging (v0.13, 1539-row manage data, terminal, session pill) all boot with 0 console errors; cross-file fns resolve. Old app.js removed; 3 HTML pages load the 7 scripts in order. README updated. Note: staging code still lives in `ui.js` — fully isolating it is A3.

items.title: Move staging-only JS into its own file + decouple from shared code
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** all staging flair (activity terminal, session pill, workspace templates, the staging resize redraw) moved from `ui.js` into a new `app/staging.js` that ONLY `staging.html` loads (inserted before `main.js`). `core.js` gained a tiny event bus (`emit`/`onEvent` over `EventTarget`); the `logAction()` calls in shared code (`data.js`, `datamanager.js`, `main.js`) were replaced with `emit('data:imported' | 'note:saved' | 'trade:deleted' | 'backup:created' | 'data:erased')`, and `boot()` now `emit('app:ready')` instead of calling `initStaging()`. `staging.js` subscribes and logs. `seedStagingIfEmpty` stays in `main.js` (boot-time data bootstrap; keeps the boot path robust). Verified in-browser: main app + demo load NO staging.js and define zero staging fns (boot fine, 0 console errors); staging's terminal logs session-ready + backup + delete via the bus. All 8 scripts pass `node --check`. Note: the staging↔index app-shell markup duplication (deferred from A1) is still open — now tracked as A5.

items.title: Keep the Store interface as the only persistence seam
items.prompt: Guardrail for future work: all reads/writes stay behind `Store` (`app/store.js`) so the planned cloud/ sync tier can drop in a `CloudStore` with the same async methods. Reject any change that calls `indexedDB` directly from app/render code.

items.title: Single-source the app shell (staging.html ↔ app/index.html ↔ demo.html)
items.doneNote: **Shipped (partial):** extended `scripts/build-includes.mjs` to inject shared app-shell partials into all three app pages via a tiny `<!--IF mode=…-->` conditional. Single-sourced into `partials/`: `app-landing.html` (app+staging), `app-scope.html` (all three), `app-filters.html` (+staging saved-filter controls via flag), `app-dash.html` (the five dashboard panels; demo journal-hint via flag), `app-modal.html` (data-manager modal; staging Saved-filters section via flag). Editing a partial + re-running the build updates all three pages. The staging-only Activity Terminal stays inline right after the shared dash include — it exists on staging ALONE, so it was never duplicated. Verified: each built page's id/data-key attribute set is identical to before; demo carries none of the staging-only nodes; the build is idempotent; all three boot in Chromium with 0 page errors (app=5 panels, demo=5, staging=6 incl. terminal + saved-filter controls). **Deferred:** the three top bars stay per-page (version badge, button sets, staging session-pill/workspace-bar diverge too much to fold safely), and demo keeps its bespoke trimmed landing + preview-only data manager.

items.title: Consolidate ad-hoc breakpoints into a named scale
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** consolidated the ad-hoc media breakpoints into a documented named scale — xs 460 · sm 560 · cal 620 · md 760 · lg 900, plus the wide min-width tiers 1100/1480/1700/1920 (full table commented at the top of `app/app.css`; one-line pointers in `site.css` + `index.html`). CSS media queries can't read custom properties without a build step, so the scale is a documented convention + snapped literals. Snapped the near-duplicates: `.cards` & `.mcards` 520→560, nav-hamburger 720→760, `.grid3` & `.price-grid` 880→900. `@media` conditions now use only {460,560,620,760,900} + the wide tiers — 520/720/880 no longer appear as breakpoints. Responsive-in-place kept (no separate mobile files). Verified in Chromium at the boundary widths (home 1280/900/560, app 560, howto 760 hamburger) — no regressions.

items.title: Use wide-display real estate deliberately (4K)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** main app + demo `.wrap` now bumps to 1360px (≥1480) / 1520px (≥1920) — dashboard fills wide displays (verified composed at 1920px, side margins ~200px vs ~370px). **Deliberately did NOT widen the marketing homepage**: bumping its container pushed body paragraphs to ~1520px (~150 chars/line), hurting line-length. Possible follow-up: widen only the homepage's grid sections (features/pricing), leaving prose narrow. On very wide screens the marketing pages and main app cap narrow while staging goes edge-to-edge. Review the max-width caps and make the main app + key marketing sections use the extra width more intentionally on ≥1480px (without hurting readability/line length). Done when: at 4K the main app and homepage feel composed, not stranded in a narrow column.

items.title: (staging) Move the session indicator next to the wordmark
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** pill now sits after the "Blotterbook v0.13" version badge; wrapped pill + popup in a relatively-positioned `.sesswrap` so the legend popup stays anchored under the pill. Verified in-browser. In `app/staging.html` the session-status pill (`#sesspill`) sits at the far LEFT of the header, before the wordmark. Reposition it to the RIGHT of the "Blotterbook v0.13" text (after the `.ver` badge) so it reads "Blotterbook v0.13 · ●Online". Keep the click-to-explain popup working and the header left-aligned. Touch the `.brand` markup + `app.css`. Done when: the online/offline/degraded pill sits just right of the version badge, popup still opens, no mobile layout shift.

items.title: (staging) Move Reset filters next to Save filter
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** Reset filters now sits immediately after Save filter (dropped `fbtn`/margin-left:auto); the trade-count floats to the right of the bar via a new `.fpush` util. Verified adjacent + count right. In the staging filter bar (`app/staging.html` + `app.css`), the Reset filters button (`#f_reset`) is separated from Save filter (`#f_save`). Relocate Reset so it sits adjacent to Save filter in the filter module. Both must keep working. Done when: Save filter and Reset filters are next to each other in the filter bar; filtering + saving still work.

items.title: Orient readers of the multi-surface app scripts
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added an orientation block at the top of `app/core.js` (7-script load order, `STAGING_PAGE`/ `DEMO_MODE` gating, shared-global-state map + "persist only via Store" note), and intent comments on the dense metrics math (realized max-drawdown walk, consecutive-streak loop, daily-PnL Sharpe) plus the equity-curve linear scales in `app/render.js`. Comments only — diff is 31 insertions/0 deletions; both files pass `node --check`.

items.title: Backlog module in the admin panel (migrate backlog.md → committed backlog.json)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** migrated `.claude/backlog.md` → committed `data/backlog.json` (26 items, all fields incl. prompts + doneNotes) via a one-time parser, then retired the `.md` to a redirect stub. Added a read-only **Backlog** section to `admin.html` that fetches the JSON and renders per-category completed/remaining count cards + an overall tally + the grouped item list (id / title / effort / status badge). Verified in-browser: 8 categories, 26 rows, "15 done · 10 remaining · 1 guardrail · 60% complete", and that the prompt/doneNote text is NOT present in the rendered admin DOM. 0 console errors.

items.title: (staging) Workspace dropdown reverts to default; remove the Default button
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** placeholder renamed "— Workspace —" → "— Default —"; selecting it now clears the saved order/collapsed state and resets to `DEFAULT_DASH_ORDER`. Removed the standalone Default button. Verified: scrambled layout reverts on selecting "— Default —". Covers two related staging requests. (1) Rename the workspace-template `<select>` placeholder from "— Workspace —" to "— Default —", and make SELECTING it revert the layout to default (today the placeholder is a no-op; the separate Default button does the revert). (2) Remove the now-redundant `#ws_default` "Default" button. Touch `app/staging.html` + the staging UI handlers (`ui.js` `initStaging` / the `ws_*` wiring). Done when: choosing "— Default —" resets module order + collapsed state to default; the standalone Default button is gone; Save layout and loading saved templates still work.

items.title: (staging) Export-report popup: preview + multi-format download + email
items.doneNote: **Shipped (staging-only):** Export report now opens an in-page modal (`#exportModal`) with a scrollable iframe preview + a format `<select>` (PDF print / PNG / JPEG / Markdown; Download disabled until a real format is picked) + Email-a-copy (mailto) + Close. PNG/JPEG rasterize the report via an SVG `<foreignObject>`→canvas (no new dependency); MD builds a markdown summary; PDF prints the iframe. `exportReport()` was refactored to share the report CSS/markup between the unchanged new-tab path (main/demo) and the modal. Verified in Chromium: preview renders, format gating works, MD + PNG downloads succeed, 0 errors; demo/main keep the new-tab export.

items.title: (staging) Edit/Delete buttons overlap the PnL column in the trades table
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** root cause was `display:flex` on a `<td>` (dropped it out of the table column model and overlapped PnL). Reverted to a normal table-cell; space the buttons with margin. Verified: action cell starts exactly at the PnL cell's right edge, no overlap. (Fix is in shared `app.css`, applies app-wide.) In the Manage-data trades table, a per-trade row's Edit/Delete action buttons visually overlay the PnL number (the actions render on top of the PnL column). Fix the table/cell layout in `app.css` (and the row markup in `app/datamanager.js` if needed) so the actions occupy their own column with no overlap. Check at staging fullscreen widths where it's most visible. Done when: PnL and the Edit/Delete actions are cleanly separated across widths; Edit/Delete still work.

items.title: (staging) Activity Terminal should fill its module (no negative space)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `.panel.term .pbody` is now a growing flex column and the terminal flexes to fill it (160px min on narrow). Verified at 1800px (3-col): terminal grows to 664px to fill its 777px panel. The Activity Terminal console (`#termwin`) has a fixed height, leaving empty space below it inside the panel on the wide/fullscreen staging grid. Make the terminal window fill its module body (flex / height:100%) so there's no gap, keeping the scroll + 200-line cap. `app.css` (`.panel.term` / `.termwin`). Done when: in fullscreen staging the terminal fills its panel with no negative space; scroll + autoscroll still work.

items.title: Second full repo audit
items.prompt: Read-only audit (NO code edits) of the current repo: architecture, code quality, duplicated code, and security posture — accounting for everything shipped since the first audit (symbol sanitization, `_headers` CSP, `tokens.css` + nav/footer partials, the `app.js` → 7-script split, screenshot downscaling). Give concrete, prioritized findings, and append any new actions to this backlog in the same format. Done when: a written, prioritized audit is delivered and new follow-ups are added to the backlog.

items.title: Roadmap rework: customer-facing, big-picture
items.prompt: Rework `roadmap.html` for a CUSTOMER audience evaluating the product — not us tracking tasks. Today it mirrors this backlog (granular/internal). Reframe into big-picture, benefit-oriented themes: shipped capabilities, what's in progress, and what's planned — feature-level, no infra/engineering minutiae. Keep the shared nav/footer/tokens (it uses the partials + `tokens.css`). The engineering backlog stays separate (this file / the F1 admin module). Done when: roadmap.html reads as a customer-facing shipped / in-progress / planned feature story, distinct from the engineering backlog.

items.title: (staging) Design review: per-day vs per-trade notes & data model
items.prompt: Design review (mostly thinking + a written recommendation; minimal code) of how per-DAY and per-TRADE data fit together. Today the calendar "day notes" is a bare comment box, while Manage data has richer per-trade notes + screenshots — inconsistent and not streamlined. Also the Manage-data "Day notes" list shows just "note" for every entry (uninformative — should preview the text/date). Propose a cleaner unified model for per-day vs per-trade notes/attachments and how each surfaces in the UI; capture the recommendation and add concrete follow-up backlog items. Done when: a written recommendation exists for streamlining per-day vs per-trade notes (incl. fixing the "note"-only day-notes list), with next-step items added to the backlog.

items.title: Review "Definitions & Caveats" for accuracy
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the only stale item was "Trade = one row … exactly as TradingView exported it" — rewrote it to "Trade = one closed position" covering auto-detection, one-row-per-close vs FIFO fills-pairing (+ hold time), and the beta caveat. Applied to all 3 app pages (identical panel). Rest verified accurate. Re-read the dashboard's "Definitions & Caveats" panel against the app's CURRENT behavior and correct anything stale — metric definitions, scope behavior, cost/tax model wording, and any staging-only features. Accuracy pass only (content). Done when: every definition/caveat matches current functionality; corrections applied.

items.title: Rename `app/index.html` → `app/app.html`
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `git mv app/index.html → app/app.html`, then added a root `_redirects` rewriting `/app/` → `/app/app.html` (200 = rewrite; the URL stays `/app/`) so the clean URL and every existing Launch link keep working with zero link churn. README updated (file-tree entry, `app.css` shared-by line, promote-staging note, and quick-start — incl. a note that the `/app/` rewrite is Pages-only, so the local static server should open `/app/app.html` directly). No code referenced `app/index.html` directly (the `../index.html` link is the homepage). Verified app.html boots in Chromium.

items.title: (staging) Keep the expanded Broker & Costs panel in view while scrolling
items.doneNote: **Shipped (staging-only):** the expanded Broker & Costs panel is now `position:sticky` while scrolling the dashboard — `display:contents` on `#landing` (when loaded) lifts `#setup` out of its short box so sticky has the full `.wrap` height. Collapsing the panel returns it to normal flow; below 760px it degrades to non-sticky so it never overlaps content. Gated by `body[data-mode=staging].loaded`. Verified in Chromium: position sticky when expanded, static when collapsed; demo/main unaffected (static).

items.title: (staging) Rework app-state flow: initial state is always the entry point
items.doneNote: **Shipped (staging-only):** staging always boots on the initial/landing state. When the isolated DB has data, `restoreSession` calls `armStagingLanding()` instead of entering the dashboard: it swaps the headline to “Start Blotterbook to access your dashboard”, updates the blurb, shows a “✓ Dashboard data already loaded — N trades” note, and enables Start; Start then opens the existing dashboard via `enterStagingDashboard()`. With no data the normal load-CSV flow is unchanged (Start gated). Main app + demo untouched (verified: demo still boots straight to the dashboard).

items.title: (staging) Make the Break-Even commission table a collapsible subsection
items.doneNote: **Shipped (staging-only):** the per-symbol commission table moved into a collapsible `<details class="csubtable">` nested under the “Commissions (all-in)” line in the Break-even panel, styled to match the “Tax — Section 1256” subsection (`.csub` + the `.cdefs` ▸/▾ marker). Collapsed shows just the all-in total; expanded shows the breakdown. Built in `render.js` gated by `STAGING_PAGE` (the standalone `#c_comm_table` is left empty in staging). Verified in Chromium: collapsible present with the table inside, `#c_comm_table` empty; demo/main keep the standalone table.

items.title: (homepage) Merge Features + Use Cases into one redesigned Features section
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the homepage Features section now leads with a horizontal row of four condensed use-cases, then a clickable feature LIST (left) + detail panel (right) that swaps on selection (first item pre-rendered for initial paint / no-JS). The standalone Use Cases section was removed and its pitch line folded in. Responsive: the use-case row collapses 4→2→1 and the explorer stacks to one column at md (760). Dropped the unused `.grid3/.fcard/.uc/.uc-grid` styles. Verified in Chromium: 4 use-cases, 6 feature items, click swaps detail + active state, single-column on mobile, 0 errors.

items.title: (admin) Strike through completed items in the backlog view
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** completed (status=done) rows in the admin Backlog list now render the title with `text-decoration:line-through`, visually distinct from open/guardrail items (which are unaffected). Verified in Chromium (computed `line-through` on done titles).

items.title: (admin) Filter the backlog by status and effort
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added Status and Effort `<select>` filters (options derived from the data) to the admin Backlog module; they combine and narrow the item LIST only — the per-category counts stay full project totals (noted inline). A Clear-filters button resets both. Verified in Chromium: 40 rows → status=done 21 → +effort=small 12 → clear 40, every shown row matching the active filter.

items.title: (staging) Review the Advanced Statistics module
items.prompt: Analyze and review the Advanced Statistics feature in staging. (a) What information can we clean up or cut? (b) What stats might customers expect that we're missing? (c) Document how the 'best/worst weekday' values are calculated and the reasoning for including them (and whether they earn their place). (d) UI cleanup: the Edge and Distribution sections are inconsistent — several entries render the main value in the MIDDLE of the entry instead of aligned like the rest; make the layout consistent. Deliver a written recommendation + apply the contained UI consistency fixes; spin off larger stat additions as their own items. Done when: a written review (cut/add/rationale) exists, the weekday calc is documented, and the Edge/Distribution layout is consistent.

items.title: Feasibility: let users choose where local data is saved
items.prompt: Explore the feasibility of letting users choose WHERE their data is saved locally. We're browser-based (data lives in IndexedDB today), so survey the realistic options and tradeoffs: the File System Access API (showSaveFilePicker / directory handles — Chromium-only, permission prompts, persistence), the Origin Private File System (OPFS), or keeping IndexedDB + explicit export/import to a user-chosen file. Note browser support, UX, and privacy implications. Deliver a written recommendation (or a clear 'blocked by browser APIs / not worth it' conclusion) + any follow-up items. Done when: a written feasibility assessment with a recommended direction exists.

items.title: Decide what business information the site needs
items.prompt: Discuss and decide what BUSINESS information needs to be added to the site — e.g. company/entity name, a business contact/address, terms of service (vs the current disclaimers), a fuller privacy policy, refund/billing terms once payments launch, and any required regulatory wording. Produce a checklist of what's needed and where it lives (legal page, footer, dedicated pages), flagging anything that's a prerequisite for monetization. Done when: a written checklist of required business info + placement exists, with must-haves-before-launch flagged.

items.title: Decide: serve the backlog via an admin-gated function vs a static file
items.prompt: `data/backlog.json` is currently a static file, so it's publicly readable (prompts included) even though the admin UI hides prompt text. Weigh moving it behind an admin-gated Pages Function (e.g. `/api/backlog` with Access + admin-key) vs leaving it static — pros/cons of each (public-repo exposure, simplicity, caching, a future write path, whether public prompts actually matter). Make a recommendation. Done when: a written pros/cons + recommendation exists (plus a follow-up item if we decide to move it).

items.title: Update the contact email to contact@blotterbook.com
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** replaced `contact@scalineaudio.net` → `contact@blotterbook.com` across the marketing pages (index/changelog/howto/roadmap/legal), the app Contact mailto buttons (`app/app.html`, `app/demo.html`), the shared `partials/footer.html`, and `LICENSE` (licensing-inquiry line). mailto subject/body params preserved. The only remaining `contact@scalineaudio.net` is this item's own task description in `data/backlog.json`, left intact as the historical record. NOTE: LICENSE's licensing-inquiry contact was switched to blotterbook.com for consistency with the done-when — revert that one line if licensing should stay on the company domain.

items.title: Retire change-plan.md (redundant with the backlog)
items.doneNote: **Verified — nothing to remove:** `.claude/change-plan.md` is gitignored and was never committed, so it is absent from the repo and this checkout (confirmed via `git log` — no history). `data/backlog.json` is already the sole committed planning source. No file change was needed; a stray local `change-plan.md` on a dev machine, if any, can be deleted by hand.

items.title: (staging) Debug why the Activity Terminal module won't collapse properly
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the staging Activity Terminal `.pbody` carried `display:flex` (0,3,0 specificity, same as the generic `.panel.collapsed .pbody{display:none}` but later in source, so flex won). Scoped it to `.panel.term:not(.collapsed) .pbody` (`app/app.css`). Verified: the terminal panel now collapses/expands like every other module.

items.title: Escape backup-restore symbol/tag option sinks (stored XSS)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** two layers. Sink: the f_symbol/f_tag filter <option> interpolations (app/data.js) are now wrapped in esc(). Boundary: Store.importAll() (app/store.js) forces trades[].root through Adapters.rootSym() and strips <>&"' from trademeta tags, so a restored backup matches CSV-imported data. Verified in Chromium: importing a backup whose symbol is a crafted img/onerror payload does NOT execute (no alert, no live element), the root is stored as sanitized text (EVILIMGSRCXONERROR...), the dropdown renders inert text, and a normal restore still completes.

items.title: Minor API/security hardening bundle (cookie/compare/headers)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** (1) the bb_staging admin-token cookie now carries ;Secure; (2) the raw-key fallback in isAdminAuthorized() uses a constant-time compare (HMAC both sides under a random key — leaks neither contents nor length); (3) /api/me is now no-store (via the shared json() helper); (4) dropped the wildcard Access-Control-Allow-Origin from /api/changelog. Verified with a Node WebCrypto test: constant-time accept/reject, me.js no-store, all function modules import.

items.title: Tighten CSP: drop script-src 'unsafe-inline' (externalize inline scripts)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** removed the last inline executable scripts so the CSP forbids inline JS. Moved each page's inline <script> to its own file (index->/assets/home.js, admin->/assets/admin.js, changelog->/assets/changelog.js); rewrote the main/demo new-tab Export report to load external /assets/report.js with per-report data in a non-executable <script type=application/json> block and addEventListener-wired buttons (it inherits the app CSP). _headers script-src is now 'self' (no 'unsafe-inline'); style-src keeps it (inline styles remain). Verified in Chromium UNDER THE EXACT DEPLOYED CSP HEADER: homepage feature explorer, admin backlog, the demo new-tab report (downloads a clean HTML file), and the staging export modal all work with ZERO CSP violations; no inline on*= handlers remain in served HTML.

items.title: Defense-in-depth: rate-limit admin writes + token out of localStorage
items.doneNote: **Shipped (defense-in-depth):** added a best-effort KV-backed fixed-window rate limiter (functions/_lib/http.js rateLimited()) keyed by Access email / client IP — /api/status + /api/config at 30/min, /api/admin-key at 20/min, 429 over limit; fails OPEN if STATUS_KV is unbound so it can't lock the admin out. assets/admin.js no longer writes the admin token (or any key) to localStorage — it lives only in the page-session field and autoKey() re-issues a fresh token each load, so an XSS can't lift a persisted credential. Verified with a Node test (35 reqs at limit 30 -> 5 blocked; fail-open without KV).

items.title: Guardrail: verify Stripe webhook signature before provisioning
items.prompt: Guardrail for the payments scaffold: never provision an account/entitlement in `functions/api/webhook.js` before verifying the Stripe signature — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW request body with `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, constant-time compare, and a timestamp/replay window. The endpoint is an inert 501 stub today; reject any future change that wires up provisioning without signature verification first.

items.title: Commission undercount: charge per contract (× qty), not per trade
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** round-turn commission is now charged per CONTRACT — `2 × per-side rate × (qty||1)` — in `costModel()` and the curve's `dailySeries()`. Close-event exports (no qty) are unchanged; fills/MotiveWave trades with qty>1 are priced correctly. The per-symbol table gained a Contracts column so `$/RT × Cts` reconciles to Commission, and the caveat copy was updated.

items.title: Non-US date + non-ET timezone assumptions misplace trades
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `normTime()` now detects D/M/Y when the first field is >12 (e.g. 25/06 → Jun 25), defaulting to US M/D/Y otherwise; a new "US dates & Eastern time assumed" caveat documents the no-timezone-conversion / ET-session assumptions. Verified by adapter tests (M/D/Y stays US, D/M/Y day>12 swaps).

items.title: IndexedDB addTrades transaction auto-commit race on large imports
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `Store.addTrades()` (`app/store.js`) was awaiting a `get()` per trade inside one readwrite transaction, which could auto-commit mid-flight on large imports (TransactionInactiveError). It now reads all ids once into a Set, then issues every `put` in a tight synchronous loop — also dedupes rows repeated within the same batch.

items.title: Adapter parsing robustness (Schwab truncation, blank rows, flip fills)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** three adapter-robustness fixes in `app/adapters.js`: `parseCSV` drops all-empty lines; the Schwab adapter skips a row with a blank optional Side cell (`continue`) instead of `break`, only stopping at a real column-count boundary; `pairFills` apportions a flip fill's realized PnL over the closed qty (not the full fill qty) so the full realized value lands on the closed contracts. +5 regression tests (60/60 pass).

items.title: Metric display polish (recovery ∞, curve subs accrual, tax weights)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** Recovery Factor shows ∞ for an all-positive curve with no drawdown (was —); subscriptions now accrue month-by-month along the equity curve instead of dropping at day 0 (endpoint total unchanged); the 1256 tax weights are normalized if loaded `state-tax.json` drifts off the 60/40 split. In `app/core.js` + `app/render.js`.

items.title: (a11y) Data-manager modal is permanently aria-hidden; no focus management
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the data-manager and staging export modals are now accessible — a shared `modalOpened`/`modalClosed` helper (`app/ui.js`) flips `aria-hidden`, moves focus into the sheet, traps Tab, and restores focus to the opener on close. Escape + backdrop close still work. Verified in Chromium (aria-hidden toggles, focus enters/restores, Escape closes).

items.title: (a11y) Keyboard + ARIA for the feature list, calendar, and curve
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the homepage feature explorer is now a real tablist (role tab/tabpanel, roving tabindex, Arrow/Home/End with selection-follows-focus, aria-live detail); calendar day cells and the equity-curve SVG are keyboard-operable (tabindex/role=button, Enter/Space; the curve adds arrow-key date navigation), and decorative `.ficon` SVGs are `aria-hidden`. Verified in Chromium (15 keyboard/ARIA assertions).

items.title: Add social/SEO meta (OG, Twitter card, canonical) + OG image
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** generated a 1200x630 raster OG card (assets/og-image.png, branded dark card). index.html gets full Open Graph + Twitter summary_large_image tags + rel=canonical; howto/roadmap/changelog/legal each get rel=canonical + per-page OG/Twitter (own title/description, shared OG image). Shared links now render a rich preview and every public page declares a canonical URL.

items.title: Add robots.txt + sitemap.xml
items.doneNote: **Audit + shipped.** A Cloudflare-managed robots.txt was already served at the edge (AI Crawl Control: `User-agent:* / Allow:/` + `Content-Signal: search=yes,ai-train=no`, and `Disallow:/` for Amazonbot, Applebot-Extended, Bytespider, CCBot, ClaudeBot, CloudflareBrowserRenderingCrawler, Google-Extended, GPTBot, meta-externalagent; no sitemap). Per decision, that managed file was DISABLED in the Cloudflare dashboard so there's one source of truth. Added a committed root `robots.txt` that subsumes the prior AI-crawler blocks + the `Content-Signal` reservation, and adds `Disallow: /app/` + `/admin.html` and a `Sitemap:` line; plus `sitemap.xml` listing the 5 public pages (matching their CH5 canonical URLs). Verified: sitemap is valid XML and its locs equal the page canonicals; robots carries the disallows + content-signal + sitemap line. NOTE: confirm on the live site post-deploy that only our file serves (the Cloudflare override is off).

items.title: Add CI/automation so the hand-run build tooling can't drift
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** .github/workflows/ci.yml (push to main + PRs) runs test-adapters.cjs, regenerates partials (build-includes) + the data manifest (build-manifest), and FAILS on any uncommitted diff — so stale partials or a stale manifest can't merge. Made build-manifest deterministic for the gate: dropped the generatedAt timestamp (nothing reads it; the app cache-busts the manifest fetch itself) and excluded backlog.json (admin-only, fetched no-store) so the manifest stops churning on backlog edits. Verified: manifest re-run = no diff, 55/55 adapter tests pass, build-includes idempotent. Note: a SessionStart hook isn't committable (.claude/ is gitignored) — the Actions workflow is the enforcement.

items.title: Single-source the version string (kill the staging.js literal + drift)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** app/staging.js reads the version from the .ver page badge instead of a hardcoded 'v0.13' in the activity-terminal 'session ready' line, so a bump no longer touches staging.js. README documents a version-bump checklist (badge = display source; reconcile config.js KV defaults + admin placeholder). Verified: the staging terminal shows 'Staging session ready · v0.13' read from the badge.

items.title: Small cleanups: stale CSS breakpoint comment + relative live-pill fetch
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the app/app.css L1 breakpoint-scale comment now references surviving selectors (.uc-row, .feat-explorer) instead of the F7-deleted .grid3/.uc-grid; assets/home.js live-status pill pings /app/ (absolute) instead of the relative app/, matching every other fetch + the _redirects convention.

items.title: Extract the duplicated functions json() Response helper
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** new functions/_lib/http.js exports a single json(obj,status,headers) helper (no-store + charset by default). config/status/admin-key dropped their byte-identical local copies; me/checkout/webhook/geo now return via the shared helper for a consistent response shape (also gives /api/me no-store). changelog keeps its bespoke long-cache headers by design. Verified: all function modules import; json() defaults + override correct.

items.title: Finish A5's deferred tail: app-topbar + app-scripts partials, demo shell
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** finished A5's tail — `app-topbar` and `app-scripts` partials now single-source the three top bars and the ordering-sensitive script block, and demo's trimmed landing + preview-only modal fold into `app-landing`/`app-modal` via a `mode=demo` branch. The three built pages are render-equivalent to before (comment-stripped/whitespace-normalized diff + 27 Chromium assertions); build stays idempotent and 100% static.

items.title: Theming hygiene: route stray hexes through tokens + AA contrast check
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** stray hex literals routed through `tokens.css` — added `--hover-line` (#33405a hover borders) and replaced on-accent text `#0d1014` with `var(--bg)`. `--faint` was failing WCAG AA on `--bg` (3.18:1) and was bumped #5b6470 → #767f8c (4.71:1); `--dim` (6.22:1) already passed.

items.title: Large-import crash: Math.max(...pnls) spread overflows the call stack
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `compute()` (`app/core.js`) now tracks best/worst as running min/max inside the existing equity-curve loop instead of `Math.max(...pnls)`/`Math.min(...pnls)` — the spread overflowed the call stack on large fills exports and blanked the dashboard. Verified a 200k-row dataset no longer throws; metrics unchanged.

items.title: Unguarded top-level prev/next handlers can kill app boot
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the calendar prev/next handlers route through the null-safe `on()` helper (`app/main.js`) instead of `$('prev').onclick`, so an absent id can't throw at module load and kill `boot()`. Verified prev/next still navigate months.

items.title: Cost inputs silently coerce blank/NaN to $0, understating costs
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `numIn()` (`app/core.js`) clamps negatives to 0 (a typed `-5` can't manufacture negative cost) and the platform-fee input gained `placeholder="0"` so an empty field reads as 0 rather than a typed value.

items.title: TradeStation auto-detect matches at the acceptance floor (over-eager)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the TradeStation adapter (`app/adapters.js`) now requires the distinctive combined `date/time` column and scores 3 (was 2, the detect floor), so a generic split-time fills export no longer auto-claims as TradeStation. IBKR also has date/time but uses `buy/sell`, so no collision. Regression test added (62/62 pass).

items.title: Keyboard focus lost on calendar/curve re-render (B10 follow-on)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `renderCalendar()` (`app/render.js`) remembers the focused day's date and restores focus to the same cell after its wholesale innerHTML rebuild, so keyboard users no longer drop to `<body>` on re-render. (The `#curve` SVG element persists across renders, so it never lost focus.) Verified in Chromium.

items.title: Admin auth / staging gate / ?check fail-open when Access env unset
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** admin-key issuance and the staging gate now **fail closed** when `ACCESS_TEAM_DOMAIN`/`ACCESS_AUD` are unset (admin-key → 503, staging → 403) unless `ALLOW_PRESENCE_AUTH=1` is set for local/preview — closing the spoof-the-header-off-Access path. The `?check` diagnostic is gated behind `ADMIN_DEBUG=1` (off by default) so it can't disclose Access config/email to anonymous callers. Prod (Access configured) is unaffected. README updated.

items.title: Pre-implementation security checklist for the Stripe/account stubs
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** added `verifyStripeSignature()` (`functions/_lib/auth.js`) — HMAC-SHA256 over the RAW body, constant-time compare, 5-minute replay window, multi-`v1` rotation support — and wired `/api/webhook` to verify before acting (no secret → 501, bad sig → 400, valid → 501 pending provisioning). Reinforced checkout/me with SECURITY notes (server-side price IDs only; authorize the session before a non-local tier). New `scripts/test-auth.mjs` (13 assertions) added to CI. Account provisioning itself stays a scaffold.

items.title: Single-source the HTML-escape helper and make it quote-safe
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the three duplicated escapers (`esc` in datamanager.js used app-wide, `esc` in changelog.js, `bkesc` in admin.js) collapse into one shared `assets/util.js` loaded first on every page. The shared `esc()` now also encodes `"` and `'`, closing the latent attribute-injection that only the strict CSP was guarding. Verified across app/changelog/admin.

items.title: Exported report hardcodes the palette (drifted from tokens.css)
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the exported report's `:root` is generated from the live token values (`getComputedStyle` at export time) instead of a hardcoded palette, and the remaining `#0d1014` literals read from `var(--bg)`/the `--bg` token. The report no longer ships the pre-L5 `--faint:#5b6470` — verified it now carries `#767f8c`, matching tokens.css.

items.title: Fold the last hand-duplicated nav/footer into the include/token system
items.doneNote: **Shipped (documented divergence):** index.html (marketing landing) and admin.html (internal tool) keep genuinely-distinct bespoke nav/footer — homepage uses section anchors + CTA/hamburger and a fuller disclaimer; admin uses a trimmed nav + 'internal' disclaimer. Folding them into the shared partials (or coupling the standalone homepage to site.css) would add risk for no gain, so each file now carries an inline note explaining why it stays bespoke, and the build-includes.mjs header is corrected (it also fixes the now-stale claim that demo keeps a bespoke shell — A6 folded it in).

items.title: Small refactor/perf cleanups surfaced by the R1 audit
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** `costModel(m)` computed once per `renderDash()` and threaded into renderCards/renderAdv/renderCalc (was ~4 walks/render); `downloadFile()` now takes a string OR a Blob (replacing `expDlBlob`); the report's headline figures are defined once and rendered as both HTML tiles and the plaintext summary; journal autosave captures date+text at schedule time (no wrong-date writes on a fast day-switch); stale `var(--red,#e0564d)`/`var(--panel,#11151b)` CSS fallbacks removed; and the CI drift gate now also catches untracked outputs via `git status --porcelain`.

items.title: (staging) Restyle the 1256 tax row as a collapsible breakdown
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** in staging, the Break-even module now shows one **Est. 1256 tax (net profit only)** headline line with the total, and the State top rate + Blended 1256 rate moved into a collapsible **Tax breakdown** details element (mirrors the F6 commission subsection). Main app + demo keep the inline rows. Values unchanged (gated on STAGING_PAGE in renderCalc).

items.title: Day-notes editor visible only when a day is selected; click-outside deselects
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the Day-notes editor (#journal) is hidden until a day is actively selected and reappears on selection; a click outside the Trading Calendar module (and outside the equity curve, the other date-selection surface) deselects the day — clears the highlight, drops the graph marker, hides the box. Capture-phase document handler so a curve re-render cannot detach the target. Verified on app/demo/staging.

items.title: (staging) Reverse the Activity Terminal so newest is on top
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** the staging Activity Terminal now prepends new lines so the most recent entry is at the TOP; the line cap trims the oldest (now last) and the view scrolls to top. Formatting/cap unchanged.

items.title: Automate two-track versioning from conventional commits + merges
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** one source of truth data/versions.json ({prod, staging}) replaces the two disconnected sources. A CI job (.github/workflows/version-bump.yml -> scripts/bump-version.mjs) bumps on merge to main — level from the conventional-commit type, track from changed paths (shared code -> both, app/staging.* -> staging, non-app -> none) — and commits versions.json back as chore(release): ... [skip ci]. Badges are runtime-fetched (assets/util.js) with the baked partial literal as offline fallback; /api/config + admin show the values read-only (manual inputs + KV versions record retired); phase derived from prod major (0.x -> Beta). versions.json excluded from the manifest; separate concerns (DB_VERSION, backup version, manifest hashes, refDataVersion) untouched. 24 bump-logic unit tests + 7 Chromium assertions pass; added to CI. README versioning section rewritten. Seed prod 0.12.0 / staging 0.22.0.

items.title: Drop prompts from Done items; keep only doneNote
items.doneNote: **Shipped:** stripped the `prompt` field from all 74 done items (kept only `doneNote`, the shipped record), and added a top-level `donePolicy` next to `commitConvention` stating that setting an item to `done` removes its prompt and keeps the doneNote (prompts stay on open/guardrail items). Net diff: 1 insertion (the policy) + 74 prompt deletions, no other changes. Verified backlog.json is valid JSON and the admin backlog panel still renders all 87 items (74 done · 11 open · 2 guardrail) with no errors — it never rendered done-item prompts anyway.

items.title: Revamp the changelog into versioned, user-facing release notes
items.doneNote: Discussed and agreed three decisions (curated source / prod track only / manual maintenance), then built. New source of truth: curated, version-keyed data/changelog.json (prod track, newest first) — each release has version, date, friendly title/summary, optional highlights; pre-versioning rolled into one beta:true 'Beta released' entry. assets/changelog.js now fetches the static file and renders versioned timeline cards (version badge, formatted date, Latest pill, summary, highlight bullets) with a small inline fallback; retired the raw-commit functions/api/changelog.js entirely. Seeded from the real chore(release) history: 0.13.0→0.14.2 plus the Beta floor. Added .entry .ver/.summary/.highlights styles to site.css; excluded changelog.json from build-manifest (fetched directly, like backlog/versions). Updated README, legal.html, and changelog.html copy off 'commit history'. Verified in-browser: 5 entries render from the live fetch, no console errors; tests 62/27/13 green, builds idempotent. Future releases: add a top entry to data/changelog.json each prod bump.

items.title: Define the staging → app/demo promotion workflow
items.doneNote: **Discussed, then documented.** Agreed three decisions: (1) version model stays independent counters — a promotion bumps prod by the commit-type level and staging keeps its lead, no numeric sync; (2) lives in README as a section + copy-pasteable checklist; (3) demo mirrors prod 1:1, with trade-mutating UI greyed out/disabled and persistence blocked (DEMO_MODE), so a promoted feature MUST appear on demo with its restrictions applied, not be excluded. Added a 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' subsection to README.md: the surface-by-gate model, the four gate layers (staging.js / STAGING_PAGE runtime guard / inline staging HTML / mode=staging partial conditional) and how to un-gate each, that staging is a proving ground where EVERY feature promotes (the activity terminal, session pill, and workspace templates are features, not dev tools), and an 8-step checklist covering gate removal, demo-restriction preservation, build-includes regeneration, three-surface verification, conventional-commit titling for the CH12 bump, and a changelog (F13) entry.

items.title: (staging) Make the 5 headline stat cards open metric detail modals
items.prompt: (staging) Make the five headline stat cards — Net PnL, Win Rate, Profit Factor, Avg Win/Loss, Max Drawdown (`#cards`, built by `renderCards` in app/render.js) — clickable. Each opens its OWN modal (reuse the `.modal`/`.sheet` pattern and the B9 `modalOpened`/`modalClosed` focus-trap helpers) showing deeper, interactive graphics/data specific to that metric — e.g. Net PnL → cumulative + gross/net/take component breakdown; Win Rate → win/loss/scratch split, by session/weekday; Profit Factor → gross profit vs gross loss, per-symbol; Avg Win/Loss → win & loss distributions; Max Drawdown → the equity curve with peak/trough markers. Staging-scoped (gate on `STAGING_PAGE`); cards stay non-interactive on app/demo. Cards must be keyboard-accessible (role=button/tabindex/Enter, per B10) and the modals accessible (per B9). Done when: activating each of the five staging cards opens a metric-specific, interactive, focus-managed, keyboard-operable detail modal, with app/demo unchanged.

items.title: (staging) Show the collapse control only when a module is full-width
items.prompt: DESIGN DISCUSSION → then build. On staging, panel collapsibility (the chevron) makes sense for full-width modules (e.g. Performance) but is arguably noise on modules that don't span the page horizontally on WIDE layouts (Trading Calendar, Break-even, Advanced Stats, Definitions & Caveats sit in the multi-column grid). Make it DYNAMIC by available width: on narrow/mobile widths those modules span full width and MUST stay collapsible; on wide multi-column layouts, hide the collapse control for non-full-width modules. Think through detection — CSS container/grid state vs a JS measure on resize — and whether to drive it purely via CSS (a container/media query that hides `.chev`) or JS. Propose the approach before building. Done when: there's an agreed rule for when the collapse control appears, the chevron is hidden for non-full-width modules on wide layouts, and collapsibility still works for every module when it spans full width (mobile/narrow).

items.title: F11: clicking inside a modal deselects the calendar day
items.doneNote: app/main.js capture-phase deselect guard now also early-returns on `e.target.closest('.modal')`, so interacting with the data-manager or export modal keeps the selected day. deselectDay() (app/data.js) flushes the debounced journal note (clearTimeout(jSaveTimer) + Store.saveJournal) before nulling selectedDate, closing the data-loss window.

items.title: Admin token leaks via the ?k= staging-launch URL (Referer/history)
items.doneNote: Kept ?k= as the navigation-safe fallback but neutered its exposure: a top-of-file IIFE in assets/util.js (loads first on every page) history.replaceState-strips ?k= immediately on load, and app/staging.html now carries <meta name="referrer" content="no-referrer"> so no subresource fetch leaks the token via Referer. Token no longer lingers in the address bar/history or any Referer header.

items.title: Harden the version-bump workflow (race, merge commits, edge cases)
items.doneNote: Hardened both halves of the CH12 automation. version-bump.yml: added `concurrency: {group: version-bump-main, cancel-in-progress: false}`, fetch-depth:0, diffs the pushed range (before..after with a HEAD~1 fallback), and wraps bump→build-includes→commit→push in a 5-attempt retry loop (reset --hard HEAD~1 + pull --rebase on rejection) so close merges can't lose a bump. bump-version.mjs: the local `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` is now guarded (try/catch → treat as no changes) so it's safe on the first/shallow commit. util.js sets window.__versionsReady = Promise.resolve(null) before its early return so the promise contract always holds.

items.title: Prod version never bumps on homepage / info-page changes
items.doneNote: Chose the 'what users see at blotterbook.com' intent. Added a PROD_ONLY set (index.html, site.css, howto/roadmap/legal/changelog.html) to classifySurfaces in scripts/bump-version.mjs so those user-facing changes now bump the prod track. Added prod-only surface cases to test-version.mjs (now 27 tests): index.html → prod only, site.css+howto.html → prod only, admin.html/README/backlog → neither, index.html+staging.js → both.

items.title: Baked .ver fallback literals drift behind the live version
items.doneNote: bump-version.mjs now calls syncBakedBadges() after writing versions.json: it regex-rewrites the baked `.ver` literals in partials/app-topbar.html (Main app/Demo → prod, Staging → staging), and the workflow runs build-includes and commits partials/app-topbar.html + app/app.html/demo.html/staging.html alongside data/versions.json. The offline fallback now tracks the live version. Synced the current literals to v0.14.1 (Main/Demo) / v0.24.1 (Staging).

items.title: Imported screenshot data URLs are unsanitized at the sink
items.doneNote: Added a cleanShots() sanitizer to importAll (app/store.js) that keeps only strings matching `^data:image/(png|jpe?g|webp|gif);base64,[A-Za-z0-9+/=]+$`, dropping any crafted shot payload at the trust boundary. Defense-in-depth: the editor now escapes the value at the render site — `` (app/datamanager.js). A malformed shot URL is now both dropped on import and inert if rendered.

items.title: Wire or clearly disable the dead admin config controls
items.doneNote: Chose the honest-labeling path (no consumer wired yet). The Feature flags section already carried the '(Scaffold — not yet consumed by the main app.)' note; reworded the Reference data section in admin.html to state plainly that the control only records a server-side timestamp in KV and has no client effect — clients cache-bust reference data via per-file hashes in data/manifest.json, not refDataVersion. No admin control now implies an effect it doesn't have.

items.title: Single-source the platform-phase (0.x→Beta) derivation
items.doneNote: Factored the phase rule into a shared classic helper `platformLabel(prod)` in assets/util.js (loads first on every page); assets/admin.js now calls it instead of its inline re-implementation. Added a 'MIRROR of platformLabel() in scripts/bump-version.mjs — keep in sync' note on the util.js copy so the Node/CI side and browser side stay aligned.

items.title: Promote Staging to Prod
items.prompt:
REPEATABLE — leave this item OPEN; never mark it done. Run to promote staging-only feature(s) onto prod + demo. Canonical procedure: the README section 'Promoting a feature from staging → prod' (the gate-layer model + 8-step checklist) — read it first; this prompt is the operational driver, the README is the source of truth.

SCOPE: promote the feature(s) named in the request. If none are named, inventory the full staging delta and promote all of it (staging is a proving ground — every feature is destined for prod + demo). Build the inventory by grepping: `STAGING_PAGE` and `if(!STAGING_PAGE)` / `if(STAGING_PAGE)` guards in `app/*.js`; the whole of `app/staging.js`; `<!--IF mode=staging-->` blocks in `partials/*.html`; inline markup in `app/staging.html` that isn't yet in a partial; and the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`. List each feature and its gate layers before editing.

PER FEATURE: (1) un-gate JS — move shared logic out of `staging.js` into its module, or delete the `STAGING_PAGE` runtime guard so it runs on every surface. (2) un-gate HTML — move inline staging markup into the right `partials/app-*.html`, or widen its `<!--IF mode=staging-->` to the modes it should reach (normally all three). (3) DEMO 1:1 — the feature MUST appear on demo, single-sourced from the SAME partial (never fork a separate demo copy — that is exactly what drifted before), with every TRADE-mutating control `disabled` in the `<!--IF mode=demo-->` path AND guarded by `DEMO_MODE`; read-only features (activity terminal, session pill) and UI-preference state (saved layouts) just mirror over. (4) if a path is no longer staging-exclusive, update the `STAGING_ONLY` set in `scripts/bump-version.mjs`.

THEN: run `node scripts/build-includes.mjs`; verify all three surfaces in a browser (prod: feature works and persists; demo: feature visible with trade-mutations disabled and nothing saved; staging: unchanged); run the gate — `node scripts/test-adapters.cjs`, `test-version.mjs`, `test-auth.mjs`, plus build-includes/build-manifest idempotency. Title the commit/PR `feat:` (or `fix:`) so CH12 bumps prod by the right level automatically — do NOT hand-edit `data/versions.json`; staging keeps its lead. Add a `data/changelog.json` (F13) entry for the new prod version describing what shipped. Commit + push (open a PR only if asked).

Each run done when: the in-scope staging features are live on prod + demo at 1:1 (demo trade-mutations disabled), all three surfaces verified, the gate is green, a changelog entry is added, and the change is committed/pushed. Leave this item OPEN (repeatable).

howToDrive: One item per prompt; one surface per prompt where possible.
Lead with the *goal*, not the mechanism; let me pick the workable path.
If you batch, name the 1–3 must-haves in case I run low on budget.
Keep using the change-plan + memory pattern for any multi-step item.
